name: Gibbard-Satterthwaite Theorem
statement: Any deterministic voting mechanism with 3+ alternatives that is surjective and strategy-proof must be dictatorial. There is no non-dictatorial, non-manipulable voting rule for 3+ candidates.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(strategy-proofness, surjectivity, non-dictatorship) → COMPLETE(universal voting mechanism) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(dictatorship or manipulability)

name: Sonnenschein-Mantel-Debreu Theorem
statement: Aggregate excess demand functions inherit almost no properties from individual rational demand. Any continuous function satisfying Walras' law and boundary conditions can be an aggregate excess demand, making equilibrium essentially indeterminate from micro-foundations alone.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(individual rationality, aggregation) → COMPLETE(determinate equilibrium) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(structure lost in aggregation)

name: Impossibility of Interpersonal Utility Comparison
statement: There is no empirically grounded, non-arbitrary method to compare utility levels across individuals. Ordinal utility is preserved under arbitrary monotonic transformation, destroying any common scale.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(ordinal preferences, individual utility scales) → COMPLETE(cardinal social welfare function) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(gauge freedom in utility representation)

name: Grossman-Stiglitz Information Paradox
statement: If markets are perfectly informationally efficient, there is no incentive to acquire costly information, but without information acquisition markets cannot become efficient. Perfectly efficient markets are impossible when information is costly.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(costly information, rational agents, market prices) → COMPLETE(perfect price efficiency) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(equilibrium requires noise traders or inefficiency rents)
known_resolutions: Accept noise traders as permanent feature

name: Revenue Equivalence Theorem Limits
statement: Under independent private values, symmetric bidders, and risk neutrality, all standard auctions yield identical expected revenue. This breaks with correlated values, asymmetric bidders, risk aversion, or collusion — where optimal auctions become intractable or non-standard.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(IPV, symmetry, risk-neutrality) → COMPLETE(revenue-equivalent auction class) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(correlation, asymmetry, or risk destroy equivalence)
known_resolutions: Allow Myerson-optimal auctions with virtual valuations

name: Condorcet Voting Paradox
statement: With 3+ alternatives and majority rule, collective preferences can be cyclic (A>B>C>A) even when every individual has transitive preferences. No Condorcet winner need exist.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(transitive individual preferences, majority rule) → COMPLETE(transitive social ordering) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(cycles emerge from aggregation)
known_resolutions: Ranked methods (Schulze, ranked pairs) resolve cycles

name: Gerrymandering Impossibility (Partisan Fairness Criteria Conflict)
statement: No single-member district system can simultaneously satisfy proportional representation, geographic contiguity, compactness, preservation of communities of interest, and competitive districts. These desiderata are mutually incompatible in general geography.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(proportionality, compactness, contiguity, community preservation) → COMPLETE(fair redistricting) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(geographic constraints force tradeoffs)
source: Various; formalized in Duchin & Tenner 2018, metric geometry of gerrymandering
known_resolutions: Accept tradeoff and optimize one criterion

name: Psychological Refractory Period / Central Bottleneck
statement: When two tasks each require central cognitive processing, the second task is delayed by a fixed bottleneck. True parallel central processing of two response-selection tasks is impossible; the bottleneck is structural, not strategic.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(task_1 central processing, task_2 central processing, simultaneity) → COMPLETE(parallel execution) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(serial bottleneck enforces queuing)
known_resolutions: Accept serial processing with task scheduling

name: Miller's Capacity Limit (7 plus/minus 2)
statement: Working memory can hold approximately 7 plus/minus 2 chunks simultaneously. This is a hard architectural limit on simultaneous information maintenance, not overcome by motivation or training (only by chunking strategy).
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(information items, simultaneous maintenance) → COMPLETE(unbounded working memory) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(capacity ceiling forces chunking or forgetting)
source: Miller 1956; refined by Cowan 2001 to ~4 chunks

name: Quine's Indeterminacy of Translation
statement: There is no fact of the matter that uniquely determines a translation manual between languages. Multiple incompatible translation schemes can be equally consistent with all behavioral evidence (the 'gavagai' problem).
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(behavioral evidence, referential semantics) → COMPLETE(unique translation mapping) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(underdetermination allows multiple incompatible schemes)

name: Hume's Is-Ought Problem
statement: No set of purely descriptive (is) premises can logically entail a normative (ought) conclusion. There is an unbridgeable logical gap between facts and values.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(descriptive premises, deductive logic) → COMPLETE(normative conclusion) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(type mismatch between descriptive and normative domains)
source: Hume 1739, A Treatise of Human Nature

name: Problem of Induction (Hume-Goodman)
statement: No finite set of observations can logically guarantee the truth of a universal generalization. Past regularities provide no deductive guarantee of future regularities. Goodman's 'grue' paradox shows even 'natural' predicates are underdetermined.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(finite observations, logical inference) → COMPLETE(universal law) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(infinite gap between finite evidence and universal claim)

name: Fisher's Fundamental Theorem Limits
statement: Fisher's theorem states mean fitness increases at a rate equal to the additive genetic variance in fitness — but this holds only for the additive component, ignoring epistasis, environmental change, frequency dependence, and drift. Real fitness landscapes are non-additive, so the theorem's 'partial result' gives an incomplete picture of evolutionary dynamics.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(additive genetic variance, mean fitness) → COMPLETE(monotonic fitness increase) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(epistasis, environment change, drift destroy additivity)
known_resolutions: Extended Price equation includes all components
Accept Fisher's theorem as partial truth about additive component only

name: Muller's Ratchet
statement: In finite asexual populations, the class of individuals with the fewest deleterious mutations can only stay the same or shrink (never grow by recombination). Once lost, the least-loaded class is gone forever, ratcheting mutation load irreversibly upward.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(asexual reproduction, finite population, deleterious mutations) → COMPLETE(purging of mutation load) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(irreversible loss of least-loaded class)

name: Competitive Exclusion Principle (Gause's Law)
statement: Two species competing for the exact same limiting resource in the same niche cannot stably coexist indefinitely; one will inevitably exclude the other. Complete niche overlap is unstable.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(two species, identical niche, limited resource) → COMPLETE(stable coexistence) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(one species driven to extinction)
known_resolutions: Predation keeps competitors below carrying capacity

name: Paradox of Enrichment
statement: Enriching a predator-prey system (increasing prey carrying capacity) can destabilize it, causing oscillations that risk extinction. More resources can paradoxically destroy ecosystem stability.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(increased resources, predator-prey dynamics) → COMPLETE(enhanced stability) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(Hopf bifurcation to oscillations and potential extinction)

name: Neural Binding Problem
statement: The brain processes different features (color, shape, motion, location) in separate cortical areas, yet we perceive unified objects. There is no single 'convergence zone' where all features combine; binding requires temporal synchrony or attention, and fails under load (illusory conjunctions).
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(distributed feature representations, unity of perception) → COMPLETE(automatic binding) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(attention bottleneck or synchrony required; illusory conjunctions under load)

name: Rate vs Timing Coding Tradeoff
statement: Rate coding sacrifices temporal precision for noise robustness; temporal/spike-timing coding sacrifices noise robustness for precision. No single neural code simultaneously maximizes information rate, temporal precision, and noise tolerance across all contexts.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(information rate, temporal precision, noise robustness) → COMPLETE(universal neural code) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(tradeoff surface forces code-switching between regimes)
known_resolutions: Multiplexed codes (rate and timing in different bands)

name: Nyquist-Shannon Sampling Theorem
statement: A bandlimited signal must be sampled at a rate of at least twice its maximum frequency to be perfectly reconstructed. Below this rate, aliasing is irreversible — the original signal cannot be recovered from its samples.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(continuous signal, discrete samples, sub-Nyquist rate) → COMPLETE(perfect reconstruction) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(aliasing folds high frequencies onto low, destroying information)
known_resolutions: Sigma-delta modulation (trade amplitude for time resolution)

name: Gabor (Heisenberg) Time-Frequency Uncertainty
statement: A signal cannot be simultaneously localized in both time and frequency. The product of time duration and bandwidth has a lower bound: delta_t * delta_f >= 1/(4*pi). Perfect time resolution implies infinite bandwidth and vice versa.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(time localization, frequency localization) → COMPLETE(simultaneous precision) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(conjugate variable uncertainty forces tradeoff)
known_resolutions: Short-time Fourier transform with window tuning

name: Shannon Channel Capacity
statement: The maximum rate of error-free communication over a noisy channel is C = B * log2(1 + S/N). This is an absolute ceiling: no coding scheme, however clever, can exceed it. Approaching capacity requires codes of infinite block length.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(bandwidth, signal power, noise) → COMPLETE(arbitrary data rate) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(noise sets hard ceiling on information throughput)
known_resolutions: Turbo codes and LDPC codes approach capacity
Spread spectrum trades bandwidth for SNR
Accept rate below capacity with practical codes

name: Ashby Material Property Tradeoffs
statement: Material properties occupy bounded regions in property space. No material can simultaneously maximize strength and toughness, or stiffness and density-efficiency, or conductivity and strength. The boundaries of Ashby plots represent fundamental tradeoff surfaces.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(strength, toughness, density, conductivity) → COMPLETE(universal optimal material) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(bonding physics forces tradeoff surfaces)
source: Ashby 1992, Materials Selection in Mechanical Design

name: VCG Budget Balance Impossibility
statement: The Vickrey-Clarke-Groves mechanism achieves truthful revelation and efficient allocation, but cannot simultaneously guarantee budget balance (no subsidies needed). In bilateral trade, no mechanism is simultaneously efficient, incentive-compatible, individually rational, and budget-balanced.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(efficiency, incentive-compatibility, individual rationality, budget balance) → COMPLETE(perfect mechanism) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(money must leak or efficiency is sacrificed)

name: Black-Scholes Model Impossibility
statement: The Black-Scholes model requires continuous trading, constant volatility, log-normal returns, no jumps, and frictionless markets. Real markets violate all of these: fat tails, stochastic volatility, discrete trading, and transaction costs make perfect hedging impossible.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(continuous hedging, constant volatility, no jumps, no friction) → COMPLETE(risk-free replication) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(real markets have jumps, fat tails, and friction)
source: Black & Scholes 1973; critiques by Mandelbrot, Taleb
known_resolutions: Accept model risk and use Greeks as approximation

name: Poverty of the Stimulus / Universal Grammar Underdetermination
statement: Children acquire complex grammar from impoverished input (no negative evidence, noisy data), suggesting innate constraints. But no proposed universal grammar fully accounts for the diversity of human languages — the parameter space is either too large or the categories too vague to be truly universal.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(finite input, no negative evidence, language diversity) → COMPLETE(single universal grammar) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(parametric variation explodes, or categories are too abstract to constrain)

name: Neutral Theory vs Selection Indistinguishability
statement: At the molecular level, much variation may be selectively neutral (Kimura). But distinguishing true neutrality from very weak selection is statistically impossible with finite population data. The null model (drift) and alternative (weak selection) generate nearly identical patterns.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(finite population data, molecular variation patterns) → COMPLETE(distinguishing neutrality from weak selection) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(statistical power insufficient to separate drift from weak selection)
known_resolutions: Functional assays to measure fitness effects directly

name: Robotic Grasping Force Closure Limits
statement: A robotic hand with n fingers can achieve force closure (resist arbitrary wrenches) only if n >= d+1 contacts in d dimensions with friction. Without friction, closure requires more contacts than degrees of freedom. No finite-contact frictionless grasp can resist all perturbations on a smooth object.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(finite contacts, smooth object, frictionless) → COMPLETE(force closure) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(wrench space cannot be spanned without friction or sufficient contacts)

name: Tripartite Language-Thought Binding Limit
statement: Wittgenstein's private language argument and the frame problem together show: meaning cannot be purely private (requires public use), purely formal (requires grounding), or purely grounded (requires compositionality). No single semantic theory achieves all three.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(private meaning, formal compositionality, embodied grounding) → COMPLETE(unified semantics) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(each approach sacrifices one leg of the triad)
source: Wittgenstein 1953; Harnad 1990 (symbol grounding); McCarthy & Hayes 1969 (frame problem)
known_resolutions: Accept partial theories for different semantic layers

name: Lewontin's Heritability Paradox
statement: High heritability of a trait within groups tells nothing about the cause of differences between groups. Variance partitioning is environment-relative: the same genotypes in different environments can reverse group rankings. Heritability is not transportable across environments.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(within-group heritability, between-group comparison) → COMPLETE(genetic explanation of group differences) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(genotype-environment interaction destroys cross-context inference)

name: Tragedy of the Commons / Social Dilemma Impossibility
statement: Individually rational exploitation of a shared resource leads to collective ruin. No arrangement simultaneously preserves open access, individual rationality, and long-term sustainability without external enforcement or self-governance institutions.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(open access, individual rationality, sustainability) → COMPLETE(optimal commons management) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(free-riding depletes shared resource)
source: Hardin 1968; formalized as N-person prisoner's dilemma

name: No Free Lunch Theorems for Optimization
statement: Averaged over all possible objective functions, no optimization algorithm outperforms random search. Any improvement on one class of problems is exactly paid for by degradation on another. There is no universally superior optimizer.
structural_pattern: COMPOSE(single algorithm, all possible fitness landscapes) → COMPLETE(universal optimizer) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(performance gains on some problems exactly offset by losses on others)
known_resolutions: Meta-learning to match algorithm to problem class
Accept that real problems occupy a small subset of all functions
